title: DaoManager._assemble_path returns empty list for root-adjacent nodes
description:
File: python/src/lichen/rpl/dao.py:189-209

The _assemble_path() method returns an empty list when the target's parent is the root:

```python
def _assemble_path(self, target: IPv6Address) -> list[IPv6Address] | None:
    chain: list[IPv6Address] = []
    node = target
    while node != self.node_address:  # stops when reaching root
        ...
        chain.append(node)
        node = parent
    return list(reversed(chain))
```

For a node directly connected to root:
- target=A, parent_map[A]=root
- chain=[A], node=parent=root
- Loop exits since node == self.node_address
- Returns reversed([A]) = [A]

This is correct. But looking at _rebuild_routes():
```python
path = self._assemble_path(target)
if path:  # Skip None (incomplete chain) and [] (target is root)
    self.routing_table.add_route(target, path)
```

The comment says [] means "target is root", but _assemble_path returns [] only if target == self.node_address from the start (immediate loop exit before any append). 

Actually, looking more carefully, if target == root (node_address), the while loop never executes, chain stays [], and reversed([]) is []. This case represents "the root is trying to route to itself" which would be pathological.

The "if path:" check filters out both None and [], which is correct. But it doesn't distinguish between "incomplete" (None) and "target is root" ([]) for logging/debugging.

This is more of a code clarity issue than a bug.
close_reason: Fixed: Improved code clarity in _rebuild_routes() by adding a docstring that documents the two skip conditions (None for incomplete/loop, [] for target-is-root). The bead title was misleading - root-adjacent nodes correctly return [node], only target==root returns []. Tests confirm behavior is correct.
